content:
Case 18:
Initial presentation:
- Age: 34
- Sex: female
- Chief complaint: Follow-up for transfusion-dependent pancytopenia with persistent severe neutropenia and sequelae of disseminated fusariosis
- Vital signs: Temp 37.7 °C, BP 104/66 mmHg, HR 102/min (regular), RR 18/min, SpO₂ 94% on room air.
Setting: Outpatient hematology follow-up in a patient with longstanding PNH/aplastic marrow failure and recent disseminated fungal infection
Capabilities: You are seeing a profoundly cytopenic, heavily transfusion-dependent patient in the outpatient clinic. You can review symptoms, exam, current medications, cytopenia pattern, and recent cross-sectional imaging. You decide on urgency and scope of inpatient versus outpatient diagnostics and whether to proceed to repeat bone marrow evaluation and extended infectious work-up.
Provide either a request for a single information item or solve.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "Absolute neutrophil count (ANC) from most recent CBC."}}

response.request: Absolute neutrophil count (ANC) from most recent CBC.

content:
Cbc Chemistry:
CBC: Hb 7.8 g/dL, WBC 0.9 × 10⁹/L, ANC 0.15 × 10⁹/L, Plt 18 × 10⁹/L; MCV 95 fL, MCH 31 pg.
Differential: neutrophils 11%, lymphocytes 64%, monocytes 19%, eosinophils 4%, basophils 2%.
Reticulocytes: 12 × 10⁹/L.
Coagulation: aPTT 35 sec, INR 1.3.
Chemistry: Na 134 mmol/L, K 4.6 mmol/L, creatinine 1.4 mg/dL, BUN 28 mg/dL, AST 41 U/L, ALT 44 U/L, bilirubin 1.6 mg/dL, LDH 328 U/L, uric acid 6.2 mg/dL, CRP 10.8 mg/dL, haptoglobin 21 mg/dL, ferritin 4950 ng/mL.
Immunology: CD4⁺ 380/µL, CD8⁺ 590/µL (CD4/CD8 ratio 0.64). Immunoglobulins: IgG 840 mg/dL (normal), IgA 68 mg/dL, IgM 42 mg/dL.

{"response": {"action": "solve", "solve": {"working_diagnosis": "Febrile neutropenia in setting of transfusion-dependent marrow failure with suspected recurrent/persistent fungal infection versus emerging hemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis (HLH)", "differentials": ["Recurrent disseminated fusariosis", "New invasive fungal infection (e.g., aspergillosis)", "Viral reactivation (CMV/EBV) with secondary HLH", "Drug-induced fever/toxicity", "Progression of marrow failure with inflammatory complication"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "Admit for inpatient management", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Start broad-spectrum IV antibiotics (e.g., piperacillin-tazobactam) and escalate antifungal coverage to voriconazole/isavuconazole", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Obtain CT chest/abdomen/pelvis", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Repeat fungal cultures (blood, respiratory if symptomatic), serum galactomannan/\u03b2-D-glucan", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Check triglycerides, fibrinogen, LDH trend, and assess for splenomegaly", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Repeat bone marrow biopsy with fungal stains and flow cytometry", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Initiate anticoagulation for PNH", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Monitor renal/liver function closely", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 3}, {"action": "Check viral PCRs (CMV, EBV, HHV-6)", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 3}, {"action": "Transfuse platelets to >20k and Hb >8g/dL", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 3}, {"action": "Consider HLH-2004 protocol if criteria met", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 3}]}}}

solve.working_diagnosis: Febrile neutropenia in setting of transfusion-dependent marrow failure with suspected recurrent/persistent fungal infection versus emerging hemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis (HLH)
differentials: Progression of marrow failure with inflammatory complication

treatment_plan.action: Start broad-spectrum IV antibiotics (e.g., piperacillin-tazobactam) and escalate antifungal coverage to voriconazole/isavuconazole
Repeat fungal cultures (blood, respiratory if symptomatic), serum galactomannan/β-D-glucan
Check triglycerides, fibrinogen, LDH trend, and assess for splenomegaly
Repeat bone marrow biopsy with fungal stains and flow cytometry
Consider HLH-2004 protocol if criteria met

content:
Round 2 begins.
Setting: Post–bone marrow work-up visit with new cytogenetic data
Capabilities: You now have bone marrow morphology, flow cytometry, histology, and karyotype results. You can integrate hypocellular marrow findings, monosomy 7, and the known PNH clone, and reassess the diagnosis and risk profile. You decide whether standard immunosuppression remains appropriate or whether clonal evolution mandates a change in strategy and transplant planning.
Request additional information or solve.

{"response": {"action": "solve", "solve": {"working_diagnosis": "Clonal evolution to myelodysplastic syndrome (MDS) with monosomy 7 in setting of PNH/aplastic marrow failure, complicated by persistent cytopenias and prior fungal infection", "differentials": ["MDS with monosomy 7 (high-risk)", "PNH clone persistence without clonal evolution", "Overlap syndrome (aplastic anemia-MDS)", "Progression to acute myeloid leukemia (AML)"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "Urgent transplant evaluation with HLA typing", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Discontinue immunosuppressive therapy (if previously administered)", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Repeat bone marrow for blast percentage and flow cytometry to confirm MDS/AML criteria", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Continue anticoagulation for PNH (e.g., eculizumab)", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Maintain transfusion support with leukoreduced/irradiated products", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Monitor for hemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis (HLH) with ferritin, triglycerides, and fibrinogen", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 3}, {"action": "Adjust antifungal prophylaxis based on prior fusariosis history", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 3}]}}}

solve.working_diagnosis: Clonal evolution to myelodysplastic syndrome (MDS) with monosomy 7 in setting of PNH/aplastic marrow failure, complicated by persistent cytopenias and prior fungal infection
differentials: PNH clone persistence without clonal evolution

treatment_plan.action: Urgent transplant evaluation with HLA typing
Repeat bone marrow for blast percentage and flow cytometry to confirm MDS/AML criteria
Maintain transfusion support with leukoreduced/irradiated products
Monitor for hemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis (HLH) with ferritin, triglycerides, and fibrinogen
Adjust antifungal prophylaxis based on prior fusariosis history

content:
Round 3 begins.
Setting: High-risk marrow failure and MDS transplant-planning discussion in the context of invasive fungal disease
Capabilities: You have full information including NGS (TET2, DNMT3A), detailed PNH clone quantification, prior IST response, and the course of disseminated fusariosis with ocular and pulmonary involvement. You must define the final diagnosis, antifungal strategy, and an allogeneic SCT plan, balancing curative intent against infectious risk and the patient's reluctance toward prolonged hospitalization.
Request additional information or solve.
Final round - provide definitive diagnosis and treatment plan.

{"response": {"action": "solve", "solve": {"working_diagnosis": "High-risk myelodysplastic syndrome (MDS) with monosomy 7 and TET2/DNMT3A mutations evolving from PNH/aplastic marrow failure, complicated by prior disseminated fusariosis", "differentials": ["MDS with monosomy 7 (high-risk)", "Therapy-related myeloid neoplasm due to prior immunosuppression", "PNH with clonal evolution to MDS", "Fungal sepsis-induced bone marrow suppression"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "Urgent allogeneic stem cell transplant evaluation with HLA typing and donor search", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Discontinue prior immunosuppressive therapy (IST) due to clonal evolution", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Continue eculizumab for PNH with thrombosis prophylaxis", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Switch antifungal to isavuconazole 200mg daily with therapeutic drug monitoring (TDM)", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Initiate outpatient transplant preparatory workup with scheduled admission for conditioning", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Maintain transfusion support with irradiated/leukoreduced products", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 3}, {"action": "Repeat bone marrow for blast assessment and minimal residual disease (MRD) testing", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 3}]}}}

solve.working_diagnosis: High-risk myelodysplastic syndrome (MDS) with monosomy 7 and TET2/DNMT3A mutations evolving from PNH/aplastic marrow failure, complicated by prior disseminated fusariosis
differentials: Therapy-related myeloid neoplasm due to prior immunosuppression
PNH with clonal evolution to MDS

treatment_plan.action: Urgent allogeneic stem cell transplant evaluation with HLA typing and donor search
Discontinue prior immunosuppressive therapy (IST) due to clonal evolution
Continue eculizumab for PNH with thrombosis prophylaxis
Switch antifungal to isavuconazole 200mg daily with therapeutic drug monitoring (TDM)
Initiate outpatient transplant preparatory workup with scheduled admission for conditioning
Maintain transfusion support with irradiated/leukoreduced products
Repeat bone marrow for blast assessment and minimal residual disease (MRD) testing
